learning_outcomes:
Gerontology 1. Major Programme: Upon programme completion,
graduates will have acquired the ability to: (1) demonstrate mastery of fundamental
concepts involved in meeting the health and social care needs of the aging
population, including - the
biological, physical, psychological, and social aspects of ageing; -
successful aging; - chronic
disease management for older people; -
community/residential care service planning; - interdisciplinary
aged care service planning, administration, coordination, and delivery; (K) (2) demonstrate the skills needed in assessing and delivering
evidence-based care for older people in the community, rehabilitative, and residential care settings; (K/S) (3) plan and coordinate aged care services within and across these
settings; (K/S) (4) critically evaluate the impacts of the changing health care,
social, and cultural contexts and contemporary policies on decision-making
for aged care service planning, coordination, evaluation, and re-engineering; (K/V) (5) assess, interpret, and apply innovative health and social care
models in evaluating and re-engineering aged care services within and across
the community, rehabilitative and residential care settings; (K/S/V) (6) work effectively with other professionals involved in aged care
service provision and apply the interdisciplinary collaborative care model in
planning and coordinating these services; (K/S/V) (7) demonstrate effective communication and
interpersonal skills in dealing with older people, their caregivers
and families, and other professionals involved in the provision of aged care
services; (K/S/V) (8) demonstrate the ability to
advocate best practices and assume a leadership role in coordinating and
fostering interprofessional practice; (K/S/V) (9) drive social innovation in
practice and lead innovation in aged care; (K/S/V) (10) exhibit professionalism in practice and adhere to legal and ethical standards of aged care; (K/S) (11) demonstrate mastery of research
skills and utilise research evidence in the provision of aged care services; (K/S) and (12) display personal and social
responsibility and engage in lifelong learning and personal development. (V) K = Knowledge outcomes
S = Skills outcomes V = Values and attitudes outcomes
